title: Vectors - Cloning and Expression
author: Garg, HK
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Cloning vectors are DNA molecules designed to carry foreign genetic material into host cells, facilitating the replication and maintenance of the introduced genes. Commonly derived from plasmids, viruses, or artificial chromosomes, these vectors act as reliable carriers, enabling scientists to amplify specific DNA sequences for various applications. 
 Expression vectors take this technology a step further. Tailored to produce proteins, these vectors contain regulatory elements like promoters, enhancers, and terminators, making sure that inserted genes are not just copied but actively expressed in host organisms. This process is vital for synthesis of proteins such as insulin and vaccines, for therapeutic use, studying gene function and protein interactions.
steps_raw:
Cloning vectors are DNA molecules designed to carry foreign genetic material into host cells, facilitating the replication and maintenance of the introduced genes. Commonly derived from plasmids, viruses, or artificial chromosomes, these vectors act as reliable carriers, enabling scientists to amplify specific DNA sequences for various applications. 
 Expression vectors take this technology a step further. Tailored to produce proteins, these vectors contain regulatory elements like promoters, enhancers, and terminators, making sure that inserted genes are not just copied but actively expressed in host organisms. This process is vital for synthesis of proteins such as insulin and vaccines, for therapeutic use, studying gene function and protein interactions.
